rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or declared as helpers in the same annex.
Resolve type mismatches by aligning helper declarations with the types of their expressions.
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scopes; use assign for output bindings instead.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all referenced identifiers are declared or visible in the same annex scope.
Use only valid AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) in const and eq declarations.
Validate that expression types match expected types in binary operations and assignments.
Prefer removing invalid clauses over adding new architecture when references cannot be resolved.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Implementation-level bindings should be placed in implementation scopes after subcomponents and connections.
Assignments in implementation scope must reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers that are not present in the model's configured dependencies.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use assign for output bindings instead.
Do not declare helpers with invalid or unresolved types; ensure all types are resolvable.
